meaning: adj.有望的,乐天的,面色红润的,血红色的,满怀希望的,指望的n.血红色,红腊笔vt.以血沾污,血染
examples: They are less sanguine about the company's long-term prospects.
He tends to take a sanguine view of the problems involved.
And Sulger is sanguine enough to keep a staff of 175 to serve 250, 000 customers.
Whereas other people have become more sanguine because of the strength in the last two years.
